background: In Exercise 3.2, you created a stemplot of lengths of hospital stays for 25 patients. Table 3.6 lists the data.

task.query: Construct a frequency table for these data using 5-day class intervals. Include columns for the frequency counts, relative frequencies, and cumulative frequencies.
task.answer:
Frequency table

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|l|l|}\hline Duration (days) & Frequency & Relative Frequency (\%) & Cumulative Frequency (\%) \\ \hline 0-4 & 5 & 20.0\% & 20\% \\ \hline 5-9 & 12 & 48.0 & 68 \\ \hline 10-14 & 6 & 24.0 & 92 \\ \hline 15-19 & 1 & 4.0 & 96 \\ \hline 20-24 & 0 & 0.0 & 96 \\ \hline 25-29 & 0 & 0.0 & 96 \\ \hline 30-34 & 1 & 4.0 & 100\% \\ \hline Total & 25 & 100\% & - \\ \hline \end{tabular}

task.query: What percentage of hospital stays were less than 5 days?
task.answer: Five of $25(20.0 \%)$ were less than 5 days.

task.query: What percentage were less than 15 days?
task.answer: Ninety-two percent were less than 15 days (see Cumulative Frequency column).

task.query: What percentage of hospital stays were at least 15 days in length?
task.answer: Two of $25(8 \%)$ were at least 15 days in length.
